speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. I see you're currently on amlodipine 10 mg once daily for hypertension, started on April 25th.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, that's right. I've been taking it every morning with breakfast.

speaker: doctor
text: How have you been feeling since starting it? Any dizziness, swelling, or unusual fatigue?

speaker: patient
text: Mostly okay, though I’ve had some mild fatigue lately — nothing severe.

speaker: doctor
text: Your fasting blood glucose today was 8.2 mmol/L — slightly elevated, but we’ll monitor that closely.

speaker: doctor
text: Your hemoglobin level came back at 176 g/L, which is within the normal range for men.

speaker: doctor
text: Your heart rate was 52 beats per minute — a bit on the lower side, though not critically so.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure reading was 87 mmHg — acceptable, but let’s keep an eye on trends.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve also had a dull headache most days this week — mostly in the temples and forehead.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your symptoms, lab results, and clinical picture, I’m diagnosing hypothyroidism — we’ll confirm with TSH and free T4 shortly.

speaker: patient
text: Hypothyroidism? That would explain the fatigue and headaches, I guess.

speaker: doctor
text: Actually, your repeat hemoglobin just came in at 180 g/L — a small but notable increase from the earlier 176 g/L.

speaker: patient
text: Is that something to worry about? I haven’t had any bleeding or other symptoms.

speaker: doctor
text: Not immediately concerning, but it does warrant follow-up — especially alongside your low heart rate and rising diastolic pressure.

speaker: doctor
text: Your latest diastolic blood pressure reading is now 107 mmHg — higher than the 87 we saw earlier today.

speaker: doctor
text: And your heart rate has dipped further to 50 bpm — consistent with possible bradycardia related to thyroid dysfunction or medication effect.

speaker: doctor
text: I’m going to switch you from amlodipine 10 mg to 5 mg once daily to help mitigate the bradycardia and blood pressure variability.

speaker: patient
text: Okay — should I take it at the same time? And do I need to check my pulse regularly?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes, same time each day. Please monitor your pulse twice daily and report if it drops below 48 or if headaches worsen.

speaker: patient
text: Got it. By the way, that blood sugar number — 8.2 mmol/L — is the same as the one you mentioned earlier.
